transcript: A gentle question of a powerful and shocking story. It is a story about a father and a son. It is a story about a terrible choice. It is a story that will stay with you long after it ends. This is Munsi Premchand's classic tale, Kafan or the Sorrowed. Our story begins in a small village. There live a father and son. The father's name is Gishu, the son's name is Madhav. They are extremely poor. They are also known throughout the village for their laziness. They avoid work whenever they can. They believe that hard work is a pointless way to live. Madhav has a wife named Budhia. She lives with them in their small hut. Budhia is the only one who does any work. She cooks all the meals. She cleans the home. She struggles to find food for everyone. She is also going to have a baby. One night, a tragedy happens. Budhia begins to have her baby. The pain is very great. She screams out for help. Ghisu and Madhav sit outside the hut. They hear her screams, but they do not help her. They do not call for a doctor. They do not find a midwife. They only feel annoyed by the noise. They do nothing but wait for the noise to stop. By the morning, the heart is silent. Budhia has died. The village elders come to the hut. The elders say that Budhia must have a proper cremation. They say her body must be covered with a new shroud. The shroud is called a kafan. Ghisu and Madhav have no money. They begin to cry and wail very loudly. They pretend to be filled with grief. The village elders feel very sorry for them. The elders collect money from the village. They give five rupees to Kishu and Madhav. The elders give the man a clear instruction. They must go to the market, they must buy a simple white cloth for the shroud. Ghishu and Madhav start walking to the market. They are holding the five rupees. This is more money than they have ever held. As they work, Gisu speaks to his son. He says that buying a shroud is a waste of good money. He says that Budhia never had a new sari when she was alive. He asks if a piece of cloth will matter to her. Madhav listens to his father, he agrees completely. The two men do not go to the cloth shop. They turn and go to the liquor shop instead. They use all the money to buy strong alcohol. They also buy delicious fried foods. They find a quiet place under a tree. They eat all the food, they drink all the alcohol. They feast until they cannot eat anymore. They drink until they cannot think clearly. They laugh and talk happily. They have completely forgotten about the dead woman in the heart. They have forgotten about the shroud. Gudhia's body remains on the cold floor. It is alone and it is uncovered. The story ends right there. The writer Munshi Premchand does not tell us what to feel, he simply shows us what happened and lets decide. The real shroud. In the story is not made of cloth. The real shroud is the deep emptiness inside Khishu and Madhav. It is a blanket of apathy that covers their hearts. They are alive, but they have lost their humanity. The story leaves us with a very difficult question. What is the true cost of poverty? When does the struggle to survive destroy our ability to care for others? There is no easy answer. The story of Cuffence asks us to find our own answer. Thank you for listening. If the story made you feel something, please let us know. Your support helps us share more important stories like this one. Please take a moment to subscribe to our channel. Tap the like button if you appreciated the summary.
